raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.0,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":0.7,"RX.4":0.0,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"rationale":{"R1.2":"The delegation to review the section centrally involves verifying the logical consistency of the argument chain presented in the artifact.","R1.3":"Assessing evidence-claim alignment is central to ensure that reported results like errors and metrics support the claims about solver performance.","R1.4":"Novelty assessment is expected in reviewing this section to estimate the delta over existing benchmarks like DETEST and Hairer–Wanner.","R1.5":"Structural reorganization is central for improving the narrative flow from introduction to protocol, results, and recommendation.","R1.6":"Writing polish at the sentence and paragraph level is a key responsibility to enhance clarity and conciseness in the technical description.","R1.7":"Citation and scholarship checks are central to verify accuracy and coverage of referenced testbanks and solvers.","RX.1":"Uncertainty disclosure review is central to identify if confidence bounds and error metrics are properly communicated.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing to ensure claims about NUMSKILL being the first fine-grained profile are not exaggerated.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is central to confirm the section stays within evaluating numerical ODE solvers without extraneous topics.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is always active but not requested or relevant here, as ODE solver evaluation poses no clear harms.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability are central to check sourcing of the 142 problems and reference computations for reproducibility."}}

rationale.R1.2: The delegation to review the section centrally involves verifying the logical consistency of the argument chain presented in the artifact.
rationale.R1.3: Assessing evidence-claim alignment is central to ensure that reported results like errors and metrics support the claims about solver performance.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty assessment is expected in reviewing this section to estimate the delta over existing benchmarks like DETEST and Hairer–Wanner.
rationale.R1.5: Structural reorganization is central for improving the narrative flow from introduction to protocol, results, and recommendation.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish at the sentence and paragraph level is a key responsibility to enhance clarity and conciseness in the technical description.
rationale.R1.7: Citation and scholarship checks are central to verify accuracy and coverage of referenced testbanks and solvers.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure review is central to identify if confidence bounds and error metrics are properly communicated.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing to ensure claims about NUMSKILL being the first fine-grained profile are not exaggerated.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is central to confirm the section stays within evaluating numerical ODE solvers without extraneous topics.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is always active but not requested or relevant here, as ODE solver evaluation poses no clear harms.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are central to check sourcing of the 142 problems and reference computations for reproducibility.
